title: Pusher Channels keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Pusher Channels App Key or App Secret detected. Pusher Channels is a real-time communication service providing WebSocket connections, pub/sub messaging, and presence channels for web and mobile applications. App keys authenticate client-side connections while app secrets authenticate server-side API requests, granting access to publish messages to channels, trigger events, query channel information, authenticate private/presence channels, and manage user presence data. Exposing these credentials enables attackers to send unauthorized messages to your channels, eavesdrop on private channel communications, impersonate users in presence channels, consume your message quota causing service disruption and unexpected costs, access sensitive real-time data being transmitted, and potentially execute denial-of-service attacks. Immediately revoke the exposed key from your Pusher dashboard at https://dashboard.pusher.com, generate new credentials, rotate all related secrets, audit recent channel activity and message logs for unauthorized access, implement server-side authentication for sensitive channels, use encrypted private channels for confidential data, and store credentials securely using environment variables or secret management systems.

Pusher Channels App Key or App Secret detected in variable assignment. Pusher Channels provides real-time WebSocket communication infrastructure for applications. This credential (app key or app secret) grants access to your Pusher application's channels including publishing events, subscribing to channels, authenticating private/presence channels, querying channel state, managing user presence, and consuming API resources. Exposing these credentials allows attackers to send malicious messages to your users, intercept private channel communications, impersonate legitimate users in presence channels, exhaust your message quota causing service outages, access sensitive real-time data streams, and launch denial-of-service attacks against your application's real-time features. Immediately revoke this credential from your Pusher dashboard at https://dashboard.pusher.com, generate replacement credentials, implement server-side channel authentication, use encrypted private channels for sensitive communications, enable webhook signatures for security events, audit all channel activity logs for unauthorized access, rotate all related API credentials, and implement secure credential storage using environment-based configuration or dedicated secret management platforms.
